reason: Adding the nosuid mount option modifies the /dev/shm filesystem configuration. While it improves security, it can disrupt applications that rely on setuid binaries in this world-writable temporary space, and the change typically requires a reboot to take effect. Because it directly alters filesystem mount behavior, it falls under the policy category for filesystem changes that are considered too risky for automated remediation.
estimated_impact: reboot required; filesystems affected; potential breakage of applications that depend on setuid/sgid binaries in /dev/shm; requires reboot to persist the change; modifies critical filesystem mount options
